acceptanceSignals: Feature is disabled by default in MVP environments.
Enabling recommendations does not remove deterministic search/filter behavior.
Recommendation failures do not block ordering flows.

attempts.latestEventSummary: feature completed: Recommendation Engine in 0 attempts

reinforcementResults.name: HTTP Contract Sync Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: The recommendation feature is correctly behind a runtime flag (`PRELAUNCH_RECOMMENDATION_ENGINE_ENABLED`) that defaults to OFF, is implemented as an additive ranking layer on top of existing deterministic filtering/sorting, and degrades gracefully when ranking fails without blocking ordering. The externally visible API changes are synchronized across canonical contract (`src/contract.rs`), published OpenAPI artifacts, generated TypeScript client models, and contract-conformance CI checks (including route-to-spec coverage and contract verification).

reinforcementResults.name: High-Risk Write Audit Discipline
reinforcementResults.summary: Recommendation capability is implemented behind a runtime feature flag that defaults OFF (`PRELAUNCH_RECOMMENDATION_ENGINE_ENABLED` parses to `false` when unset), applied only as an additive ranking layer after deterministic filtering/sorting, and guarded so recommendation errors degrade gracefully without blocking ordering. This feature adds no new high-risk write path, while the existing immutable audit model in the workspace already enforces actorId/role/entityId/reason/timestamp/correlationId capture with append-only evidence sequencing.

reinforcementResults.name: No Legacy Path Rule
reinforcementResults.summary: The recommendation engine feature satisfies the active policy: it is gated by a runtime flag defaulting to OFF, recommendation is applied as an additive ranking step after deterministic filtering/sorting, recommendation failures degrade to deterministic ordering without blocking order creation, and superseded compatibility paths (recommendation query DTO/parameter) were removed in the same feature batch.

reinforcementResults.name: Optional Feature Flag Discipline
reinforcementResults.summary: The current recommendation feature satisfies the active policy: it is gated by a runtime flag (`PRELAUNCH_RECOMMENDATION_ENGINE_ENABLED`) parsed with default `false`, recommendation is applied only as an additive ranking step after deterministic filtering/sorting, and recommendation failures explicitly degrade to deterministic ordering without blocking ordering flows (covered by dedicated tests for flag-off behavior, deterministic ranking, and failure isolation).

reinforcementResults.name: Data Retention Enforcement
reinforcementResults.summary: The workspace satisfies the Recommendation Engine feature requirements and the active retention/deletion policy: recommendation is runtime-gated default OFF, applied only as additive ranking with deterministic fallback, failures do not block ordering, and payroll/order/audit retention workflows (scheduled purge paths, encryption/access-control coverage, and audit-query traceability of purge outcomes) are implemented with test evidence.

excludedApproaches: Replacing core filtering with recommendation-only ranking.
Introducing separate source-of-truth state for recommendation outputs.
executionConstraints: Ship behind runtime feature flag default OFF.
Treat recommendation as additive ranking layer only.
Preserve deterministic discovery behavior when flag is OFF.
name: Recommendation Engine
rationale: Recommendations are phase-2 value add and must not destabilize MVP.
summary: Provide optional recommendation capability behind controlled rollout.
